scenes.text:
The average person spends four hours a day on
their phone. That is sixty days a year.

Sixty days. Two entire months every year, gone. Scrolling through other people is highlight reels while your own life sits on pause. Social
media companies hire the smartest engineers in the world to keep you addicted. You are not weak. You are fighting a billion-dollar machine.

Set a screen time limit. Put your phone in another room when you work. Delete the apps
that steal your time. This is not about willpower. It is about designing your environment for success.

The phone is a tool. If it is using you
more than you use it, that is a problem.

Check your screen time right now. Share
your number. Follow for digital discipline.
